hooks.command: cmd=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if printf "%s" "$cmd" | grep -qE "\b(grep|egrep|rg|ripgrep)\b"; then jq -n "{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:\"PreToolUse\",additionalContext:\"cx-nudge: this Bash call uses grep/rg. For code navigation (symbol definitions, references, callers) prefer the cx tool (see the cx skill) per CLAUDE.md. If this is a plain-text search cx cannot do, ignore this.\"}}"; fi

jq -r '(.tool_input.new_string // .tool_input.content // "") as $new | (.tool_input.old_string // "") as $old | ($new / "\n" | map(select(test("(^|[^:])//|/\\*|^\\s*\\*")))) as $nc | ($old / "\n" | map(select(test("(^|[^:])//|/\\*|^\\s*\\*")))) as $oc | ($nc - $oc) as $added | if ($added | length) > 0 then {hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PostToolUse", additionalContext: "comment-audit: this edit added or changed comments. Per CLAUDE.md, resist comments that explain what you just did — keep a comment only if a reader with no knowledge of this conversation would be UTTERLY CONFUSED without it. Delete any that narrate the change or restate what the code plainly does."}} else empty end'
